algorithms.title: Suspected Stroke Field Recognition and Transport Decision
algorithms.description: Sequence for recognizing suspected stroke, ruling out a correctable mimic, and deciding on transport destination.
steps: Identify a new focal neurologic deficit (current or recently resolved) using a validated stroke scale (e.g., CPSS: facial droop, arm drift, speech abnormality).
Check blood glucose immediately; treat hypoglycemia per protocol if present and reassess neurologic status.
Determine and precisely document the last-known-well time from the patient/bystanders.
Assess for LVO-suspicious findings (gaze deviation, severe combined face+arm weakness, neglect/denial) using a local LVO-screening scale if available.
Provide early hospital notification ('stroke alert') so the receiving facility can prepare.
Transport per local destination protocol — nearest stroke-capable hospital, or bypass to an endovascular-capable center if LVO screen is positive and system design supports it.
Reassess neurologic status serially en route without delaying transport for an extensive exam.
